headline: Built a full-funnel measurement framework in Amazon Marketing Cloud
context: Designed an end-to-end clean-room framework showing how campaigns move a global pet-nutrition brand from awareness to loyalty — feeding client budget decisions.

detail.overview: A global pet-nutrition brand needed a consistent way to see how its advertising drove the full customer journey — awareness, consideration, purchase, and loyalty — across its main product lines in Japan.
detail.problem: Reporting was fragmented and hard to trust, so the team couldn't confidently reallocate budget.
actions: Designed a privacy-safe clean-room measurement framework in Amazon Marketing Cloud covering the whole funnel and all product lines
Engineered the SQL and a BigQuery pipeline that organized 240+ products into clean categories and produced consistent, reconcilable metrics
Built a reusable Python visualization toolkit and a documented style guide so every chart was brand-consistent and presentation-ready
impact: Gave the client one trustworthy full-funnel view to guide budget allocation
Standardized reporting into reusable templates that cut turnaround and rework for the team

headline: Ran a clean-room experiment that cut wasted ad frequency for a spirits brand
context: Designed a controlled pre/post test to answer whether removing a frequency cap helped or hurt media efficiency — and turned it into a clear recommendation.

detail.overview: A premium spirits brand had removed the frequency cap on its awareness advertising and wanted to know whether that was a good idea.
detail.problem: Ad frequency builds up over time, so a simple before/after comparison would be misleading — the answer needed a controlled design.
actions: Designed a controlled pre/post experiment with matched time windows to isolate the cap's true effect
Measured reach efficiency and cost-per-reach with privacy-safe aggregation
impact: Showed that uncapping nearly doubled frequency but cut unique reach by 26% and raised cost-per-reach
Recommended re-applying the cap, projected to roughly halve cost-per-reach on awareness media

headline: Measured new-customer acquisition and audience overlap to redirect budget
context: Quantified how much advertising actually wins new customers and whether retargeting was set up correctly — reframing the metrics so the client could act.

detail.overview: Clients needed to know whether their advertising was bringing in genuinely new customers, and whether retargeting across channels was working.
detail.problem: The obvious metrics were misleading — a rate-based view and a single-month window both pointed the wrong way and risked a bad budget call.
actions: Built a customer-level new-to-brand analysis and reframed it from a misleading rate to absolute new-customer volume
Analyzed audience overlap across Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads and corrected the measurement window to reflect how retargeting builds over time
impact: Showed advertising drives nearly all new-customer acquisition, and flagged where budget was over-indexed on existing customers
Turned a 'is retargeting broken?' escalation into clear evidence it was working — guiding reallocation toward new-customer growth

headline: Took CIDER's paid media in-house and tripled performance
context: Rebuilt Naver & Kakao paid media end to end after agency results fell ~70%.

detail.overview: CIDER's Korea paid media was outsourced to an agency whose ROAS had fallen ~70%. Over two years I brought the entire Naver & Kakao operation in-house and rebuilt it from measurement up — tripling ROAS.
detail.problem: Agency-run Naver & Kakao campaigns were down ~70% in ROAS, and the numbers couldn't be trusted: broken UTM parameters meant the in-house site analytics and the ad platforms' reported results didn't reconcile, so budget was being steered on conflicting data.
actions: Transitioned Naver & Kakao paid media fully in-house over two years, owning campaign structure, bidding, and creative
Diagnosed and rebuilt the tracking layer — fixed the broken UTM parameters so in-house site data and ad-platform results reconciled, restoring a single source of truth for performance
Ran systematic A/B tests on creative and targeting, cutting underperformers and scaling the winners
impact: ROAS +200% YoY (3x) after in-housing
+26% MoM average ROAS growth, sustained across the period
Tracking rebuilt so site analytics and ad platforms reconciled, with decisions driven by trustworthy data

headline: Reworked CIDER's Naver KOL playbook for +67% GMV
context: Built sponsorship guidelines, an influencer tier chart, and metrics from scratch.

detail.overview: CIDER's influencer marketing on Naver ran without structure, internal SOPs, or reliable tracking.
detail.problem: Naver results are hard to track directly, and there was no repeatable playbook, so each campaign started from zero.
actions: Wrote detailed sponsorship guidelines so influencers produced varied, on-brand content
Built an influencer tier chart and defined performance metrics in Excel
Followed up individually with each blogger to recover campaign results Naver couldn't track
Negotiated using cross-channel mirrored exposure instead of just higher fees
impact: +67% GMV versus the previous KOL campaign
Repeatable SOPs and tiering adopted across Naver, Instagram, and YouTube

headline: Built CIDER's ambassador program to 9x sales on budget
context: Ran it end to end as PM — 20+ styled looks, tiered rewards, ad-ready content.

detail.overview: CIDER, a fast-fashion e-commerce brand, had little local awareness or loyalty in the Korea market.
detail.problem: The brand needed deeper engagement, a reason for local shoppers to explore the app, and a steady supply of advertising content.
actions: Designed the program around three goals: engagement, an in-app exclusive collection, and ad content
Prepared 20+ head-to-toe styling looks, finalized through manager and team feedback
Coordinated with logistics for a final check two weeks before each shoot
Set reward tiers across categories (most reviews, most content, best reels) and routed content to ad and design teams
impact: Sales reached 9x the invested budget
Content hit 52% for feed and 47% for reels, surpassing initial targets

headline: Ran CIDER's Seoul pop-up to 3x competitors' footfall
context: Led event, viral marketing, press, and agency contracts on a lean budget.

detail.overview: Ahead of pop-ups at Waterbomb and Konkuk University, CIDER launched a large pop-up at Space Wadiz, Seoul.
detail.problem: The event had to make a big splash on a tight budget, and offline pop-up ROI is hard to measure.
actions: Sent well-written press drafts to a curated journalist list to earn coverage without paid placement
Researched other pop-up stores for format and merchandising ideas
Built a data-tracking framework and aligned KPIs across teams
Worked through ROI from viral marketing, offline footfall, and SNS participation
impact: +7,243% surge in SNS search volume
About 3x the venue's usual visitor rate, and 3x competitors' average footfall

headline: Grew CIDER's Naver Blog +81% as the brand entered Korea
context: Built Instagram-style content and ran interim customer service before support existed.

detail.overview: With no recognition in Korea, CIDER needed reach and an early customer-service outlet before formal support existed.
detail.problem: Limited manpower, no brand awareness, and rising customer questions all landed at once — and the blog had to feel like an extension of the brand.
actions: Engaged influencers directly by commenting on their blogs to build relationships
Created Instagram-style Naver content (trend roundups, styling guides, vacation picks)
Posted idol-inspired coordinated looks on Twitter to reach style-driven audiences
Ran exchange, return, sizing, and FAQ posts as interim customer service
impact: +81% daily visitors to the Naver Blog
Follower count doubled within six months

headline: Lifted CIDER's address conversion +20% with localized UX
context: Specced an auto-address API, reworked sizing, and mapped the full refund journey.

detail.overview: CIDER's app lacked Korean localization and local-standard UX for sizing, address entry, and returns.
detail.problem: Foreign-centric sizing and a manual address flow created friction for Korean shoppers, and changes needed C-level buy-in.
actions: Wrote the dev document for an automatic address-fill service and integrated it with the backend team
Ran an A/B test with and without the address API
Benchmarked domestic e-commerce sizing and proposed localized options
Mapped the full return/refund journey stage by stage against competitors and surfaced weak points
impact: +20% address conversion from the automatic address API
Localized sizing reduced related customer inquiries
Leadership accepted the gaps and moved forward on improvements

headline: Localized CIDER's CRM on KakaoTalk to lift retention
context: Integrated Kakao Alimtalk and a chatbot, and wrote CS SOPs for overseas teams.

detail.overview: CIDER's CRM relied only on email and in-app push, with no localized channel — while Korean e-commerce leans heavily on KakaoTalk.
detail.problem: There was no localized CRM channel, and no Korean-language customer service for local shoppers.
actions: Integrated Kakao Alimtalk messages: payment reminder (30 min), split shipping, order confirmation, review request
Synced templates with the internal tech team
Planned and built a Kakao customer-service chatbot and wrote localized CS SOPs for overseas teams
Mapped 10+ competitors across fashion e-commerce, fashion brands, and SPA brands for comparison
impact: Higher retention and payment-success rates after the Kakao messages went live
Improved local CS quality through SOPs
Chatbot reduced direct inquiries and collected complaint data

headline: Turned CIDER customer FGIs into a product action plan
context: Planned and ran focus group interviews end to end as PM.

detail.overview: As a customer-centric team, CIDER needed real Voice of the Customer from domestic users to guide product and marketing.
detail.problem: There was no structured way to capture in-depth feedback on UI/UX and product from Korean customers.
actions: Planned and ran focus group interviews end to end as PM
Collected detailed feedback on UI/UX and product offerings
Synthesized findings into a prioritized action plan
impact: Customer feedback turned into a concrete, prioritized action plan for the team

headline: Modeled a multinational's market share in Power BI
context: Portfolio project on a sample dataset — Power Query cleaning and DAX modeling.

detail.overview: A portfolio project analyzing a multinational electronics manufacturer (VanArsdel) against competitors using a sample benchmarking dataset.
detail.problem: Five separate tables (sales, products, manufacturers, geography, dates) needed cleaning and modeling before any insight was possible.
actions: Cleaned and reshaped data in Power Query (fill-down, split columns, append, conditional columns, type fixes)
Built table relationships and a date table, and hid unnecessary tables
Wrote DAX measures: % Growth, PY Sales, total Sales, and Market Share
Used grouping to show only the top competitors and added filter-based interactions
impact: Mapped VanArsdel at 48.55% market share with 10.94% average growth (2017-2021)
Surfaced that the US drove 52.73% of revenue while Canada was underpenetrated — a localization opportunity

headline: Found the drivers behind a 26.86% telecom churn rate
context: Portfolio project — Excel pivot analysis across demographics, plans, and geography.

detail.overview: A portfolio project analyzing customer churn for a US telecom (Databel sample dataset) to surface drivers and propose actions.
detail.problem: Churn was rising in a competitive market, but the reasons weren't broken down by who was leaving or why.
actions: Cleaned the data using a structured checklist and built a churn-rate column for pivot analysis
Categorized churn reasons and grouped ages into Under 30 / Senior / Other
Cross-analyzed churn by data plan, contract length, and state with international activity
impact: Quantified overall churn at 26.86%, mostly lost to competitors with better devices/offers
Isolated high-risk segments: seniors, unlimited-plan users under 5GB, and international users in California

headline: Built an interactive Excel/VBA sales dashboard
context: Portfolio project — 5,782 rows with slicers, VBA controls, and theme switching.

detail.overview: A portfolio project to push Excel data-visualization skills, building an interactive dashboard from a US company's sales data.
detail.problem: A 5,782-row dataset across 10 categories needed to be explorable by non-technical viewers, not just static charts.
actions: Built pivot-based waterfall, stacked bar, line, map, combo, and doughnut charts
Used GETPIVOTDATA for real-time text callouts and added slicers for filtering
Coded VBA interactive buttons, info tabs, color-theme switching, and on/off toggles
impact: Delivered a fully interactive dashboard covering revenue, delivery, returns, and satisfaction
Surfaced clear reads like 67% on-time delivery and a 10% return rate

headline: Segmented customers into 5 actionable groups in Python
context: Portfolio project — compared K-Means, DBSCAN, PCA, and GMM; chose a 5-cluster GMM.

detail.overview: A portfolio project building a customer-segmentation model on a public marketing dataset so campaigns could be targeted by behavior.
detail.problem: A heterogeneous customer base had heavy overlaps, so raw charts gave no clean segments to act on.
actions: Ran feature engineering (grouped age, marital status, family size, expenses, engagement) and dropped outliers
Compared K-Means, hierarchical clustering, DBSCAN, PCA, and GMM
Selected a 5-cluster GMM for finer granularity at an acceptable silhouette score (0.1342)
Mapped each cluster to a marketing play (discounts, BNPL, VIP, cashback, household deals)
impact: Produced 5 clearly defined, action-ready customer segments
Confirmed a positive income-to-spend relationship to prioritize high-value targets

headline: Analyzed 39 job posts to rank in-demand analyst skills
context: Self-collected 39 international Tokyo postings and analyzed them in Excel.

detail.overview: A self-directed project analyzing what international employers in Tokyo actually expect from data and business analysts.
detail.problem: Skill expectations were anecdotal — there was no data-backed view of what to prioritize learning.
actions: Collected 39 Data/Business Analyst postings, focused on international companies
Coded and counted required skills and tools in Excel
Ranked programming languages and visualization tools by frequency
impact: Found SQL required most often (ahead of Python/R), with visualization second — Tableau leading, then Power BI, Excel, Qlik, Looker
Confirmed bilingual ability and a bachelor's degree as baseline, with domain knowledge and communication as differentiators
